summary: Postauricular lymphadenopathy in kids is mostly due to general infection sources; while sore throat can be involved, it isn't exclusively so but represents part of broader infectious etiologies frequently seen among young patients experiencing such symptoms regularly throughout their growth stages. The primary driver appears linked closely enough to these everyday illnesses affecting large segments of childhood populations justifying selection for 'sore throat' over other possibilities presented
